ingredients: rice, chicken, carrot, green bean, cabbage, egg, shallot, garlic
ingredients_original: 4 cups white rice, 1 piece chicken breast (cut into small pieces), ½ stick carrot (diced), 4 stalks green beans (sliced), 1 cup cabbage (finely sliced), 2 eggs, 4 cloves shallot, 4 cloves garlic, 2 tablespoons cooking oil, 1 tablespoon sesame oil, 1 teaspoon white pepper powder, 1 teaspoon salt, A little fried onion
cooking_steps: Heat the oil. Sauté the shallot and garlic until golden. Add the chicken and cook briefly. Add the carrot, green beans, and cabbage, stirring briefly. Crack in 2 eggs. Add the white rice and mix well. Season with salt, white pepper, and sesame oil. Sprinkle with fried onions as a garnish. Ready to serve.
tips: No specific tip provided.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: spaghetti, onion, garlic, anchovy, spinach
ingredients_original: 250 g spaghetti (boiled until cooked), 1 holland onion (diced), 5 cloves garlic (finely chopped), 2 tablespoons anchovies (soaked, rinsed and drained - coarsely pounded), 2 tablespoons chili flakes, 1 teaspoon white pepper powder, 4 stalks fresh parsley leaves, 1 cup spinach (cut into small pieces), 3 tablespoons olive oil, ½ teaspoon salt
cooking_steps: Boil the spaghetti in boiling water first. Heat the olive oil over medium heat. Sauté the yellow onion and garlic until golden. Add the anchovies, chili flakes, white pepper, and salt, then cook until golden. Add the spaghetti and mix well. Add a little water. Add the parsley and spinach. Stir evenly. Ready to serve.
tips: No specific tip provided.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: lemongrass, chili, shallot, garlic, candlenut, ginger, dried shrimp, hardtail scad
ingredients_original: 4 stalks lemongrass, 8 red chilies, 4 shallots, 2 cloves garlic, 2 candlenuts, 1 teaspoon sugar, ½ inch ginger, 1 teaspoon dried shrimp, 1 teaspoon salt, 4 calamansi limes (squeezed juice), 1 cup water, 4 hardtail scad fish, 2 tablespoons margarine
cooking_steps: Stuff the finely blended ingredients into the cleaned fish. Add margarine to the ingredients. Wrap the fish in banana leaves. Grill the fish until cooked. Ready to serve with white rice and your favorite vegetables.
tips: No specific tip provided.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: bread, egg, tomato, lettuce
ingredients_original: 8 slices wholemeal bread, 4 chicken eggs (boiled), 1 tomato (thinly sliced), 2 lettuce leaves (cut into small pieces), 2 full tablespoons low-fat mayonnaise, ½ teaspoon white pepper powder
cooking_steps: Mash the boiled eggs. Add white pepper powder and mayonnaise, mixing evenly. Spread the egg mixture onto the bread. Place the cucumber, tomato, and lettuce slices. Cut into two halves. Ready to serve.
tips: Wholemeal bread is more nutritious and higher in fiber compared to white bread. You can use boiled chicken as a substitute for eggs. Using commercial pepper powder (lada sulah) is not recommended because it contains high sodium; replacing it with pure white pepper powder is recommended. The best choice is using low-fat mayonnaise.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: shrimp, squid, fish, chili, shallot, onion, garlic, lemongrass, tomato, galangal, mushroom, cauliflower, baby corn, carrot, celery
ingredients_original: ½ cup shrimp, 1 cup squid, ½ cup fish fillet (cleaned), 1 liter (4 cups) water, 4 red chilies, 4 shallots, 2 onions, 2 cloves garlic, 2 stalks lemongrass, 2 tablespoons cooking oil, 1 tablespoon ground chili, 1 ripe tomato, 2 stalks lemongrass (crushed), 1 inch galangal (sliced), 2 kaffir lime leaves, 4 cloves garlic (crushed), 1 holland onion (cut into eight), 4 bird's eye chilies (crushed), 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon sugar, 4 limes (squeezed juice), 1 tomato (cut into 4), 1 cup oyster mushrooms, 1 cup cauliflower @ broccoli (cut into small pieces), 4 baby corn, 1 carrot (cut lengthwise), 1 stalk celery (cut into small pieces), Coriander leaves (a little)
cooking_steps: Heat the cooking oil. Sauté the blended ingredients over medium heat. Stir until fragrant and crisp. Add water and let it boil. Add the garlic, lemongrass, yellow onion, and galangal. Add the seafood and simmer for 20 minutes. Season with salt and sugar. Add the vegetables, kaffir lime leaves, coriander leaves, and squeezed lime juice. Stir well. Ready to serve with rice or blanched vermicelli.
tips: Add coriander leaves to enhance the cooking aroma. The recipe can use other protein sources such as chicken, meat, or crab according to your preference.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: chicken, carrot, green bean, chili, garlic, onion, ginger, lemongrass
ingredients_original: 4 pieces chicken (breast part, cut into small pieces), 2 tablespoons cooking oil (for sautéing), 1 carrot (cut long), 1 cup green beans, 2 green chilies (sliced), 2 teaspoons turmeric powder, ½ teaspoon fennel powder, ½ teaspoon cumin powder, 1 teaspoon salt, 3 cloves garlic, 1 onion, 1 inch ginger, 2 stalks lemongrass (crushed)
cooking_steps: Mix the chicken with turmeric powder, salt, fennel, and cumin. Marinate for 30 minutes. Heat the cooking oil. Sauté the onion, garlic, ginger, and lemongrass until fragrant. Add the marinated chicken and fry until cooked. Add the vegetables and fry until tender. Ready to serve with white rice and selected vegetables.
tips: Use a non-stick pan to reduce oil usage. Increase the use of herbs and spices like onion, ginger, turmeric powder, fennel, and cumin to substitute salt and enhance the chicken's flavor.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: oat, milk, yogurt, chia seed, mango, banana
ingredients_original: ½ cup oats (instant or quick cooking), 1/3 cup low-fat milk, 1/3 cup low-fat yogurt, 1 tablespoon chia seeds, 1 tablespoon honey, 1 tablespoon desiccated coconut, 1/3 cup ripe mango (diced), ½ ripe banana (sliced)
cooking_steps: Put the oats into a glass. Pour in the low-fat milk. Add the honey, desiccated coconut, chia seeds, yogurt, banana, and ripe mango. Store in the refrigerator overnight.
tips: No specific tip provided.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: mackerel, okra, dried shrimp, chili, shallot, garlic, candlenut
ingredients_original: 4 medium-sized mackerel fish, 4 okra (sliced diagonally), 1 tablespoon dried shrimp (soaked first), 2 kaffir lime leaves, ½ lime (squeezed juice), 1 teaspoon sugar, ½ teaspoon salt, Banana leaves and aluminum foil, 4 red chilies, 4 shallots, 4 cloves garlic, 4 candlenuts, 1 tablespoon chili powder, 2 tablespoons cooking oil, 1 cup water
cooking_steps: Heat the cooking oil. Sauté the blended B ingredients until fragrant and crisp. Line the aluminum foil with banana leaves. Place the fish on the banana leaves. Top the fish with sambal paste, kaffir lime leaves, and sliced okra. Squeeze lime juice over it. Wrap the fish neatly. Grill for 15-20 minutes. Ready to serve with white rice and selected vegetables.
tips: No specific tip provided.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: chicken, chili, garlic, lemon
ingredients_original: 600g chicken fillet, 1 red chili (finely cut), 2 cloves garlic, ½ lemon (squeezed juice), 2 tablespoons honey, 1 teaspoon sugar, ½ teaspoon salt, 100ml water, 2 teaspoons corn flour (mixed with ½ cup water), ½ lemon (squeezed juice), 2 cloves garlic (finely pounded), 1 teaspoon white pepper powder
cooking_steps: Marinate the chicken with B ingredients for 30 minutes. Bake the chicken in the oven (150°C) for 30 minutes. Add water into a pan followed by squeezed lemon juice. Add the red chili, garlic, salt, sugar, and honey, stirring evenly. Finally, add the diluted corn flour mixture and stir until thick. Remove the chicken from the oven and pour the lemon sauce over it. Ready to serve with white rice or pasta and selected vegetables.
tips: No specific tip provided.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: oat, wheat flour, rice flour, chili, onion, ginger, yogurt
ingredients_original: ½ cup oat cereal (rolled or instant), ¼ cup wheat flour and ¼ cup rice flour, 2 green chilies (finely sliced), 2 tablespoons onion (finely chopped), ½ teaspoon ginger, A little coriander leaves, A little curry leaves, ¼ cup low-fat yogurt, 1¼ cups water, ½ teaspoon cumin powder, ¼ teaspoon salt, A little cooking oil
cooking_steps: Blend the oat cereal, wheat flour, rice flour, and low-fat yogurt with water. Pour the batter into a bowl. Add the onion, curry leaves, green chili, coriander leaves, cumin, ginger, and salt. Beat slightly until well combined. Heat a pan with a little oil. Pour 1 small ladle of batter onto the pan and spread it out. Cook until the bottom is crisp and golden. Ready to serve with dhal gravy.
tips: Using a non-stick pan helps reduce oil usage. Using herbs like cumin, ginger, curry leaves, and coriander leaves enhances the flavor and aroma of the dosa.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: egg, chicken, chili, shallot, garlic, onion, tomato, vegetable
ingredients_original: 4 chicken eggs, 1 piece chicken breast, cut into small pieces, ½ cup cooked water, 2 tablespoons dried chilies, 4 shallots, 4 cloves garlic, 1 onion (diced), 4 ripe tomatoes (diced), ½ cup mixed vegetables (frozen mixed vegetable), ½ teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon sugar, 2 tablespoons cooking oil
cooking_steps: Heat the cooking oil. Sauté the blended ingredients until crisp. Add the chicken and stir until cooked. Add the water, vegetables, and salt, mixing until fragrant. Crack the eggs into the sauce one by one. Let it simmer until the eggs are cooked. Ready to serve with white rice and selected vegetables.
tips: Sodium content is reduced by using fresh tomatoes as the main sauce ingredient instead of chili sauce, oyster sauce, or tomato sauce. Use only fresh chicken and avoid processed meats. The eggs are not fried first, but directly added into the boiling gravy to reduce oil usage.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: mushroom, tomato, corn, carrot, seaweed, shrimp, macaroni, anchovy
ingredients_original: ½ cup fresh shiitake mushrooms (sliced), 1 tomato (cut into 4 or 6), 1 corn stalk (cut into 4), ½ carrot (diced), 1 gram dried seaweed, 1 cup fresh shrimp meat, 1 cup dried macaroni, 1 tablespoon anchovies, 1 teaspoon white pepper powder, ½ teaspoon salt, 4 cups water
cooking_steps: Boil the water. Add the corn, carrot, tomato, mushroom, and dried macaroni. Simmer until cooked. Add the shrimp, anchovies, white pepper, salt, and seaweed. Cover and let it simmer until cooked. Ready to serve while hot.
tips: Using ingredients containing natural "glutamate" such as mushrooms, tomatoes, fish, shrimp, tofu, and seaweed can enhance the savory "umami" taste without using MSG or stock cubes.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: bread, tomato, onion, mushroom, broccoli, cheese, egg, milk
ingredients_original: 2 slices wholemeal bread, ½ cup tomato (diced), ½ cup onion (chopped), ½ cup fresh mushrooms, ½ cup broccoli (cut into small pieces), 2 slices cheddar cheese, 3 eggs, ¼ cup low-fat milk, A little white pepper powder, ¼ teaspoon salt, A little parsley leaves, A little soft unsalted margarine
cooking_steps: Place the wholemeal bread pieces into a baking pan greased with margarine, pressing them down until compact. Add the onion, broccoli, mushroom, and tomato. Mix the eggs with white pepper, salt, and low-fat milk, beating until well combined. Pour the egg mixture over the ingredients in the pan. Tear and sprinkle the cheese slices on top. Add the parsley leaves. Bake at 200°C for 20-25 minutes. Ready to serve.
tips: No specific tip provided.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: barramundi, onion, garlic, chili, ginger, lemongrass, lime
ingredients_original: 1 small-sized barramundi (cleaned and scored), 1 holland onion (sliced in rounds), 4 cloves garlic (chopped), 5 bird's eye chilies (crushed), 2 inches ginger (sliced long), 2 stalks lemongrass (thinly sliced), 4 limes (juice extracted), ¼ teaspoon salt, ½ teaspoon oyster sauce, 1 teaspoon corn flour, ½ cup water, A little coriander leaves, kaffir lime leaves, red chilies and lime slices
cooking_steps: Stuff the lemongrass into the fish's stomach. Squeeze a little lime juice over it. Sprinkle with ginger, lemongrass, garlic, yellow onion, kaffir lime leaves, bird's eye chilies, and red chilies. Mix the corn flour, oyster sauce, salt, water, and lime juice, stirring until well combined. Pour the mixture over the fish. Steam the fish for 15-20 minutes. Garnish with coriander leaves and red chilies. Ready to serve with white rice and selected vegetables.
tips: No specific tip provided.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: rice, onion, garlic, anchovy, chili, ginger flower, lemongrass, green bean, cabbage, chicken, cucumber
ingredients_original: 4 cups white rice, ½ onion, 2 cloves garlic, 2 tablespoons anchovies (washed and coarsely pounded), 2 bird's eye chilies, 1 torch ginger flower, 1 stalk lemongrass, 2 kaffir lime leaves, ½ cup green beans, ½ cup cabbage, 1 piece chicken breast (cut into small pieces), 4 tablespoons cooking oil, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon white pepper powder, A little cucumber (thinly sliced)
cooking_steps: Heat the oil. Sauté the onion and garlic until golden. Add the chicken and cook until done. Add the anchovies, bird's eye chilies, lemongrass, torch ginger flower, kaffir lime leaves, green beans, and cabbage, stirring evenly. Add the rice and mix well. Season with salt and white pepper. Ready to serve. Garnish with sliced cucumbers.
tips: Green beans can be substituted with winged beans or long beans. Cabbage can be replaced with red cabbage or sliced carrots to increase antioxidants and add appealing colors. Using herbs like lemongrass, torch ginger flower, and kaffir lime leaves enhances the flavor and aroma of the kerabu rice.
source_book: Resipi Masakan: #SedapTakSemestinyaMasin

ingredients: wonton wrapper, chicken, prawn, water chestnut, carrot, egg, shallot, ginger
ingredients_original: 16 wonton wrappers, 120 g (½ cup) chicken meat fat and skin removed and minced, 5 g (3 pieces) water chestnut roughly chopped, 30 g (¼ cup) carrot finely sliced, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) soy sauce, 1 egg lightly beaten, 3 g (1 teaspoon) corn flour, 1.5 L (6 cups) homemade chicken stock, 40 g (4 pieces) shallot finely sliced, 10 g (½ inch) ginger sliced, Salt and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Mix chicken, prawn, water chestnut, carrot, corn flour and soy sauce. Mix the filling ingredients well and leave for 30 minutes. Place 1/2 teaspoon of filling in the center of the wonton skin. Gather the 4 corners of the wonton skin to the center and seal by brushing with beaten egg. Boil 3 cups of water in a pot. Once boiling, place the wontons into the water. When the wontons float to the surface, scoop them out of the pot and set aside. Simmer the chicken stock with shallots and ginger for a few minutes. Sprinkle a little white pepper and a pinch of salt. Turn off heat and serve wontons with soup and noodles.
tips: “Wonton” comes from two Chinese words that literally mean “small dumpling”. In Cantonese, it means “swallowing clouds”.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: wholemeal flour, cauliflower, onion, green chilli, spring onion, ginger
ingredients_original: 200 g (2 cups) wholemeal flour/bread flour, 150 ml (¾ cup) water, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, 112 g (1 cup) cauliflower finely chopped, 50 g (½ cup) onion chopped, 1 green chilli thinly sliced, 10 ml (2 teaspoons) lemon juice, 2 stalks fresh spring onion sliced, 1 inch ginger chopped, Salt to taste, Oil for cooking
cooking_steps: Sift flour with salt in a large bowl and mix with cooking oil using fingers. Add 3/4 cup water little by little, adding more water if necessary. Knead until the dough becomes soft and elastic, cover and leave for 30 minutes. To make the filling, place cauliflower in a bowl and pour enough hot water to cover the surface. Cover bowl and leave for 5 minutes. Strain the softened cauliflower by pressing slightly to remove as much water as possible. In a bowl, mix cauliflower, lemon juice, salt, spring onion, onion, chilli and ginger. Mix well and divide into 6 parts. To make paratha, knead each dough into a thin pancake. Spoon 1 part of the cauliflower mixture onto the pancake and fold in half, pressing the edges to seal the filling. Shape into a ball again. Knead the dough again carefully to become flat about 5 inches wide and 1/4 inch thick, without opening the filling. Heat and grease a flat pan with a little oil. Fry the paratha until both sides are cooked and golden brown. Serve with dhal curry.
tips: Cauliflower is a cruciferous vegetable containing phytochemicals that help the body fight diseases. For a complete meal, you can serve this recipe with Thai Style Vegetable Curry (pg 71) and Tandoori Chicken (pg 52).
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

original_recipe_name: Lempeng Mil Penuh dengan Stew Epal
ingredients: wholemeal flour, self-raising flour, milk, egg, apple, yogurt
ingredients_original: 25 g (¼ cup) wholemeal flour, 125 ml (½ cup) self-raising flour, 125 ml (½ cup) milk, 1 egg, 5 g (1 teaspoon) butter softened, 100 g (1 piece) green/red apple, 125 ml (½ cup) water, 7 g (1 teaspoon) sugar, 14 g (1 tablespoon) butter, A pinch of cinnamon powder, 30 g (2 tablespoons) low-fat yogurt
cooking_steps: Peel the apple, remove seeds and cut apple into small sizes. Melt butter in a pot and pour water with sugar. Boil and add apples. Simmer covered until the apples are soft. Sprinkle a little cinnamon powder over the apple stew and set aside. Sift wholemeal flour and self-raising flour into a large bowl and make a hole in the center of the flour mixture. Mix milk, butter, egg, and fold into the flour and beat slowly to make a soft batter. Place a little butter on a flat pan and pour the batter and cook until air bubbles appear. Flip the pancake to cook on the other side. Serve pancake with apple stew and yogurt.
tips: Apples can be replaced with other fruits like pears, honeydew melon or bananas. Choose wholemeal wheat flour because whole grain products are high in fiber, vitamin B and minerals. It provides the overall goodness of whole grains. You are recommended to include whole grains in half of your daily meals.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: egg, tuna
ingredients_original: 3 eggs, 10 g (1 teaspoon) tuna flakes in water, Salt and white pepper to taste, A little vegetable oil
cooking_steps: Mix all ingredients and beat until combined using a fork or chopsticks. Do not beat too hard to avoid bubbles. Heat a pan with a little oil. Place 2-3 full tablespoons of egg mixture into the pan. Cook over medium heat until slightly set but not too runny. Roll using a fork or chopsticks to the front of the pan. Place another 2 tablespoons of egg mixture into the pan. Spread around the pan surface and lift the rolled egg slightly so the uncooked egg flows underneath. Cook until the layer is almost hard, then roll that egg together with the already rolled egg in the opposite direction. Add a little more oil and repeat steps until all eggs are used. Place the cooked egg on a plate with the last fold at the bottom. Roll tightly using kitchen tissue to absorb excess oil. Let the whole roll cool at room temperature. Cut using a sharp knife and serve with wholemeal bread and fresh salad.
tips: For a quick and healthy complete breakfast, serve the rolled egg with fresh salad, cherry tomatoes and wholemeal bread. Bento is a single-portion takeout or home-packed meal in Japan. A traditional bento consists of rice, fish or meat with one or more types of cooked vegetables, usually arranged in a box-shaped container.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: lentil, carrot, brinjal, cucumber, tomato, shallot, garlic
ingredients_original: 100 g (1 cup) dhal soaked overnight and boiled until soft, 60 g (½ cup) carrot cut into small cubes, 86 g (1 cup) brinjal cut into small pieces, 50 g (½ piece) cucumber halved and cut into small pieces, 25 g (1 piece) tomato cut into small pieces, 800 ml (3 cups) homemade vegetable stock, 10 g (1 piece) shallot chopped, 10 g (2 cloves) garlic chopped, 10 g (1 teaspoon) curry powder, 20 g (2 teaspoons) chilli powder, 1 stalk curry leaves, 5 g (1 teaspoon) cumin, 20 g (2 teaspoons) black mustard seeds, 7 g (1 teaspoon) turmeric powder, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) vegetable oil, 2 tablespoons coriander leaves sliced
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add shallots, garlic and sauté until fragrant. Then add curry powder, turmeric powder, mustard seeds, chilli powder, curry leaves and cumin. Sauté briefly and stir constantly to avoid burning. Add vegetable stock. Add dhal beans into the pan and boil until the gravy becomes thick. Add carrot and brinjal and continue boiling until cooked. Then add cucumber and finally tomato. Cover and boil over small fire for 15 minutes until dhal beans are tender and gravy becomes thick. Serve with coriander leaves.
tips: Suitable to be served with thosai, naan, chapati or rice. Dhal is a legume that is a good source of protein and fiber. Vegetarians can consume this dish with other grains like whole grain bread to get the benefits of complete protein. This vegetarian dish is an excellent way to reduce saturated fat intake, especially from meat and chicken.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: popiah skin, carrot, cabbage, soybean, shallot
ingredients_original: 3 pieces spring roll wrappers (square), 180 g (2 cups) grated carrot, 130 g (1½ cups) cabbage finely chopped, 430 g (2½ cups) soybeans soaked overnight and boiled until soft, 10 g (1 piece) shallot finely chopped, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) tomato paste, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) soy milk, 10 ml (2 teaspoons) oil, 10 ml (2 teaspoons) sesame seeds, Salt and white pepper to taste, A pinch of cinnamon powder
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pan and sauté onion until fragrant and crisp. Add carrot and cabbage. Cook for 1-2 minutes. Blend soybeans in a blender and add to the pan. Add tomato paste, salt, white pepper and cinnamon powder to the pan. Turn off heat and let cool. Cut popiah skins in half and divide the filling into 6 parts. Place a spoonful of filling lengthwise at the end of the popiah skin. Brush soy milk at the end opposite the filling. Then roll to seal the filling. Repeat this step until filling is used up. Cut rolls into 6 equal lengths and place on a tray greased with butter. Brush the top of each roll with soy milk and prick with a fork. Sprinkle a little sesame seed. Bake over medium heat 210°C for 20-25 minutes, or until crispy and browned.
tips: Soybeans are legumes that are a good alternative to meat, and they are low in fat and high in protein, fiber, and complex carbohydrates. When buying canned beans, look for brands with no added salt.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: rice vermicelli, prawn, bean sprout, carrot, tofu, onion, garlic, dried shrimp, egg
ingredients_original: 300 g rice vermicelli soaked until soft and drained, 200 g fresh prawns peeled, 200 g bean sprouts, 150 g carrot cut into small long strips, 3 pieces tofu steamed and thinly sliced, 3 g (½ teaspoon) black pepper, 40 g (4 pieces) shallot, 20 g (4 cloves) garlic, 10 g (2 tablespoons) dried shrimp, 15 g (2 tablespoons) oyster sauce, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) oil, 1 piece omelette sliced, Spring onion for garnish, Salt and white pepper to taste, A little water, Garnish: 2 limes halved
cooking_steps: Heat oil and sauté blended ingredients until fragrant. Add prawn, salt, oyster sauce and a little water. Mix ingredients until combined and continue cooking. Add carrot and cook until soft. Then add rice vermicelli and stir well. When rice vermicelli is soft, add bean sprouts. Add salt and white pepper to taste. Add sliced tofu and mix well before serving. Garnish with sliced omelette, spring onion and lime.
tips: You can replace rice vermicelli with whole wheat pasta, spinach pasta, or angel hair pasta. Grain-based products like rice vermicelli have a high carbohydrate content as an energy source. If possible, use whole grain rice vermicelli.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: honeydew melon, oat cereal, yogurt
ingredients_original: 300 g (3 slices) honeydew melon cut into small cubes, 75 g (1 cup) oat cereal toasted until crispy, 190 ml (¾ cup) low-fat yogurt, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) strawberry jam, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) watermelon juice
cooking_steps: Divide cut honeydew melon between 2 tall glasses and set aside extra for garnish. Place oat cereal on top of fruits in an even layer and end the layer with low-fat yogurt. Mix jam and watermelon juice in another container and spread over the yogurt. Finally, garnish with the set aside honeydew melon cubes and serve immediately.
tips: This recipe is also suitable for children (the child portion size is half of this serving size). If your child only likes one type of fruit, encourage them to enjoy a variety of other fruits like bananas, mangoes, and so on through this recipe. Consume at least 2 servings of fruits daily. This recipe can encourage your family members to consume more fruits every day.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

recipe_name: Asparagus Salad with Low Fat Dressing
original_recipe_name: Salad Asparagus dengan Sos Salad Rendah Lemak
ingredients: asparagus, mixed legume, celery, onion, tomato, garlic, yogurt
ingredients_original: 50 g (5 stalks) asparagus hard stem and thick skin removed and cut short, 425 g (2 cups) dried mixed legumes (black beans, black-eyed peas, chickpeas, soybeans, red beans) soaked overnight drained and rinsed, 40 g (2 stalks) celery finely chopped, 25 g (1 piece) onion finely chopped, 75 g (3 pieces) tomato roughly chopped, 10 g (3 tablespoons) parsley for garnish, 45 ml (3 tablespoons) olive oil, 10 ml (2 teaspoons) vinegar, 5 g (1 clove) garlic crushed, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) tomato chutney/puree, Salt and black pepper to taste, 113 g (¾ cup) natural yogurt, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) lime juice, 60 g (¼ cup) honey, ¼ teaspoon grated lime zest, 10 g (1 piece) shallot finely chopped, 10 g (2 cloves) garlic crushed, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) lemon juice, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) persone juice, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) cider vinegar, 60 ml olive oil, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) cider vinegar, 5 ml (1 teaspoon) honey, Salt and black pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Mix all sauce ingredients in a bowl and leave for a while or chill in the refrigerator for 1 hour. Boil all types of legumes in boiling water until soft. Drain and rinse with cold water. Mix asparagus, celery stick, onion, tomato and mix until combined. Serve legume salad with its sauce and garnish with parsley leaves.
tips: It is recommended to soak dried beans and grains in room temperature water for a few hours to shorten boiling time. It also helps dissolve phytic acid (an anti-nutrient). Before soaking, discard discolored or defective beans and any foreign matter. Legumes and grains are also nutritious protein sources besides meat, fish and chicken. They also function as a source of fiber and other nutrients like iron and zinc.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: water spinach, long bean, bean sprout, cucumber, yam bean, tofu, tempeh, egg, shallot, garlic, peanut
ingredients_original: 150 g (1 bunch) water spinach cut, 75 g (5 stalks) long beans cut into 1 inch length, 150 g (1½ cups) bean sprouts, 150 g (½ piece) cucumber diced, 100 g (1 cup) yam bean diced, 300 g (2 blocks) firm tofu cut into cubes and grilled, 140 g (2 pieces) tempeh cut lengthwise and fried with a little oil, 3 hard boiled eggs quartered, 2.5 cm shrimp paste lightly toasted, 40 g (3 tablespoons) chilli paste, 50 g (5 pieces) shallot, 15 g (3 cloves) garlic, 80 g (½ cup) peanuts roasted and ground, 1 cm small sand ginger pounded, 125 ml (½ cup) tamarind juice, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, Salt and sugar to taste
cooking_steps: Blanch long beans, bean sprouts and water spinach in boiling water until moderately soft. Keep blanching water and set aside. To make peanut sauce, heat oil in a pan and sauté blended ingredients until browned and fragrant. Add chilli paste, sauté briefly and then add shrimp paste. Continue sautéing until fragrant and oil separates. Add 2 cups of vegetable blanching water with sand ginger and stir well. Add tamarind juice, stir and simmer until gravy thickens. Then add ground peanuts. Mix well and add salt and sugar to taste. Turn off heat and let sauce cool. Scoop out oil floating on top of the gravy. Arrange vegetables including cucumber with tempeh, tofu and egg on a serving plate. Pour sauce over it and serve.
tips: Other ingredients can be added or substituted such as cassava leaves, fiddlehead ferns and potatoes.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: dried beancurd skin, cauliflower, broccoli, carrot, button mushroom, yam bean, shiitake mushroom, garlic, chinese celery
ingredients_original: 6 pieces dried beancurd skin (6x6 inch size) soaked until soft and wiped, 60 g (½ cup) cauliflower cut into small pieces, 50 g (½ cup) broccoli cut into small pieces, 108 g (½ stalk) carrot cut lengthwise, 3 pieces button mushroom drained and thinly sliced, 100 g (1 cup) yam bean sliced, 11 g (5 pieces) shiitake mushroom thinly sliced, 10 g (2 cloves) garlic chopped, 1 bunch chinese celery roots removed and cleaned, 7 g (1 tablespoon) oyster sauce, 180 ml (¾ cup) homemade chicken stock, 7 g (1 tablespoon) corn flour mixed with ½ cup homemade chicken stock, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, Salt and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pan and sauté garlic until fragrant. Then add carrot, followed by cauliflower, broccoli, yam bean, shiitake mushroom and button mushroom. Sauté briefly. Pour chicken stock with oyster sauce and simmer over medium heat for 10 minutes. Add salt and white pepper to taste. Finally, add corn flour mixture and turn off heat. Set aside and let cool briefly. Divide into 6 parts. Spread out 1 piece of dried beancurd skin. Place 1 part of cooked vegetables on the beancurd skin sheet and wrap neatly and carefully so the skin does not tear. Tie neatly using chinese celery stalk. Repeat this step until all vegetables are used. Place vegetables wrapped in dried beancurd skin in a steamer and steam for 5 minutes. Serve while hot.
tips: You can replace some ingredients or add more ingredients like tofu, cabbage, glass noodles, green beans and firm tofu (taukwa) into the dish for a variety of colors and protein. Beancurd skin (fucuk) is a soy product that contains protein. This dish is one way to add legumes into your diet.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: bamboo shoot, carrot, soft tofu, dried black mushroom, dried black fungus, yellow capsicum, egg, ginger
ingredients_original: 1 L (4 cups) homemade chicken stock, 25 g (½ piece) bamboo shoot cut lengthwise, 25 g (¼ piece) carrot cut lengthwise, 25 g (¼ piece) soft tofu cut into cubes, 13 g (6 pieces) dried black mushroom soaked and thinly sliced, 3 pieces dried black fungus soaked and sliced, 30 g (½ medium piece) yellow capsicum diced, 1 egg beaten, 20 g (2 teaspoons) chilli powder, 5 ml (1 teaspoon) soy sauce, 10 ml (2 teaspoons) vinegar, 40 g (2 tablespoons) tomato sauce, 5 ml (1 teaspoon) sesame oil, 5 g (2 teaspoons) corn flour dissolved in ½ cup water, 2 stalks spring onion chopped, 1 inch fresh ginger grated, Salt sugar and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Boil chicken stock and add chilli powder, tomato sauce, soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, salt, ginger, sesame oil and white pepper. Add bamboo shoots, carrot, capsicum and black fungus into the soup. Cook over medium heat until vegetables are tender. Add black mushroom and corn flour into the soup, stirring constantly until it becomes thick. Pour beaten egg slowly and continue stirring. Cook for a few more minutes, then turn off heat. Finally add cut tofu and transfer to a serving bowl. Garnish with spring onion and serve while hot.
tips: This dish is nutritious because it contains different types of vegetables. Hot and sour vegetable soup is a type of dish originating from Sichuan, China. According to Chinese culinary writers, Sichuan cuisine consists of seven basic flavors: sour, spicy, hot, sweet, bitter, aromatic, and salty.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: tofu puff, carrot, brinjal, long bean, red capsicum, onion, lemongrass, torch ginger flower, milk, coconut milk
ingredients_original: 70 g (5 pieces) tofu puff halved, 108 g (1 medium piece) carrot sliced diagonally, 400 g (3 pieces) brinjal sliced diagonally, 75 g (5 pieces) long beans cut into 1 inch length, 60 g (1 piece) red capsicum sliced, 50 g (2 pieces) onion chopped, 14 g (2 tablespoons) curry powder, 6 g (1 teaspoon) turmeric powder, 5 g (½ teaspoon) chilli powder, 30 g (1 stalk) lemongrass pounded, 70 g (1 stalk) torch ginger flower thinly sliced, 500 ml (2 cups) low-fat milk, 250 ml (1 cup) thin coconut milk, 500 ml (2 cups) water, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, 1 kaffir lime leaf sliced, 1 stalk coriander leaves chopped, Salt to taste
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pan and sauté onion until fragrant. Then, add torch ginger flower, curry powder, turmeric powder and chilli powder. Sauté for about 3 minutes. Add vegetables, starting with carrot, long bean and brinjal. Stir vegetables until slightly soft. Then, add water, capsicum and continue cooking. Slowly pour in milk and coconut milk. Boil the curry, and add lemongrass and kaffir lime leaf. Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes. Add water if necessary and salt to taste. Just before turning off heat, add tofu puff. Sprinkle coriander leaves before serving.
tips: Reduce the use of coconut milk and replace half of its quantity with milk, or reduce its thickness by adding water.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: brinjal, shallot, onion, bird's eye chilli
ingredients_original: 260 g (2 large pieces) brinjal halved, 50 g (5 pieces) shallot, 13 g (½ piece) onion, 5 bird's eye chillies, 25 g (2 tablespoons) chilli paste, 125 ml (½ cup) water, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) lime juice, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, Salt and sugar to taste
cooking_steps: Grill brinjal on a flat pan over slow fire until slightly soft. Set aside. Heat oil in a pan and sauté pounded ingredients until browned and fragrant. Add chilli paste and continue sautéing. Add water, stir and simmer. Add salt and sugar to taste. When sambal is cooked and oil separates, turn off heat. Squeeze lime juice and stir well. Pour sambal over brinjal and serve with rice.
tips: Brinjal can be replaced or cooked with lady's fingers (okra). The purple color of brinjal makes the dish more attractive. The oil separated from the sambal can be discarded to reduce fat and oil intake.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

recipe_name: Cassava Leaves Cooked with Fermented Durian
ingredients: cassava leaf, anchovy, stink bean, lemongrass, milk, coconut milk, fermented durian paste, bird's eye chilli
ingredients_original: 200 g (½ bunch) cassava leaves boiled and water discarded, 1 piece turmeric leaf thinly sliced, 60 g (½ cup) anchovy, 75 g (1 pod) stink bean separated from stalk and halved, 60 g (2 stalks) lemongrass thinly sliced, 250 ml (1 cup) low-fat milk, 125 ml (½ cup) coconut milk, 60 g (½ cup) fermented durian paste, 8 bird's eye chillies, 1 cm fresh turmeric, 125 ml (½ cup) water, Salt to taste
cooking_steps: Heat a pan over medium heat and add cassava leaves, turmeric leaf, lemongrass, stink bean together with blended chilli and turmeric mixture. Then pour in coconut milk and milk. Mix well until leaves shrink slightly. Add dried anchovies and continue cooking over medium heat. Add water and simmer until gravy thickens slightly. Add fermented durian paste and mix well. Add salt to taste and continue stirring until gravy becomes thick and slightly dry. Turn off heat and serve with rice.
tips: Blanching cassava leaves before cooking can soften the leaves to ease digestion, besides removing cyanide content which is toxic to the human body. Therefore, cassava leaves must be blanched separately and the water discarded.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: chicken breast, red capsicum, onion, garlic, ginger
ingredients_original: 400 g (2 pieces) skinless chicken breast cut into medium pieces, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) tomato sauce, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) honey, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oyster sauce, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) vinegar, 7 ml (½ tablespoon) soy sauce, 60 g (1 piece) red capsicum thinly sliced, 26 g (1 piece) onion thinly sliced, 10 g (2 cloves) garlic crushed, 10 g (½ inch) ginger grated, 20 g (2 tablespoons) sesame seeds toasted, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) sesame oil, 125 ml (½ cup) water, Corn flour enough to coat chicken, Salt and black pepper to taste, 2 stalks spring onion chopped
cooking_steps: Mix salt and black pepper in a bowl. Add chicken pieces and mix well. Cover and marinate for about 30 minutes in the refrigerator. Coat chicken with corn flour and fry with a little oil until cooked and golden yellow. Remove from pan and use kitchen tissue to absorb excess oil. Heat pan with sesame oil. Sauté onion, garlic and ginger until fragrant. Add oyster sauce, tomato sauce, soy sauce, honey, vinegar and capsicum over medium heat. Add a little water and cook until boiling while stirring. Simmer until sauce thickens. Coat chicken with sauce in the pan or in a serving dish and garnish with spring onion and sesame seeds.
tips: Toasted sesame oil is dark, has a stronger smell and is more fragrant compared to regular sesame oil. It is usually used in Chinese cooking. Use only a little in cooking. Brushing chicken with a little oil and roasting it in the oven is better than frying.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: mutton, ginger, garlic, green chilli, red chilli
ingredients_original: 500 g (6 shoulder pieces) lean mutton, 90 ml (6 tablespoons) lemon juice, 7 g (2 tablespoons) garam masala, 5 ml (1 teaspoon) blended ginger, 20 g (2 cloves) garlic crushed, 5 ml (1 teaspoon) dried red chilli crushed, 5 g (1 teaspoon) brown sugar, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) honey, 3 stalks coriander leaves chopped, 1 green chilli finely chopped, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) oil, Salt and white pepper to taste, Garnish: red chilli slices
cooking_steps: Prick mutton meat several times with a fork, rinse, dry and set aside. In a large bowl, mix ginger with lemon juice, garlic, crushed dried red chilli, garam masala, salt, sugar, white pepper and honey. Coat mutton meat with marinade and marinate for about 1 hour. Preheat oven. Place mutton on a rack with a tray under the rack. Sprinkle coriander leaves and chopped green chilli over mutton. Pour remaining marinade and drizzle a little oil over mutton. Roast mutton in preheated oven for 10-15 minutes at 180°C, turning occasionally until cooked and browned. Transfer to a serving container and garnish with sliced red chilli.
tips: Garam masala is a blend of several types of dry spice powders. It contains 10-12 different spices such as cumin, coriander, cloves, cardamom, black pepper, and nutmeg. It is usually added to food at the last step or right before serving to enhance the food's flavor. Mutton is placed on a rack in the oven so excess oil drips into the tray, thus reducing the amount of fat consumed. Garam masala can be found in selected shops, retail markets, and supermarkets.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: chicken, garlic, ginger, yogurt
ingredients_original: 2 kg (1 whole) chicken skin and fat removed quartered, 40 g (4 cloves) garlic chopped, 40 g (2 inches) ginger finely blended, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) lemon juice, 250 ml (1 cup) plain yogurt, 3 g (1 teaspoon) garam masala, 3 g (1 teaspoon) coriander powder, 3 g (1 teaspoon) cumin powder, 3 g (1 teaspoon) turmeric powder, 3 g (1 teaspoon) curry powder, 3 g (1 tablespoon) sweet paprika, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) mint sauce (optional), 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, Salt and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a small pan over medium heat. Add coriander, cumin, turmeric, garam masala, curry powder, mint sauce and paprika. Sauté until fragrant (about 2-3 minutes). Set aside. Mix the cooled spice ingredients into yogurt. Slowly add lemon juice, garlic, salt and ginger. Prick chicken meat slightly with a knife (to the bone) 3-4 parts of chicken leg/thigh pieces. Coat chicken with marinade, cover and leave for at least an hour (preferably 6-8 hours or overnight). Preheat oven to 240°C. Transfer chicken pieces to a baking tray. Bake for about 20-25 minutes or until chicken is cooked brown on top. Chicken is cooked when clear juice flows out. Leave for at least 5 minutes before serving with mint sauce. Serve with naan, roti paratha, or steamed rice.
tips: Serve with rice and other vegetable dishes for a complete meal. Cooking with various spices will add flavor to the dish and can reduce the use of salt. Yogurt is also added to the marinade because it is rich in calcium.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: beef, lettuce leaf, shallot, garlic, ginger
ingredients_original: 500 g beef fat removed and thinly sliced, 60 g (6 pieces) crispy lettuce leaves, 10 g (1 piece) shallot, 20 g (4 cloves) garlic, 3 g (1 teaspoon) black pepper, 10 g (½ inch) ginger, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) thick soy sauce, 10 ml (2 teaspoons) sesame oil, 10 g (1 tablespoon) sesame seeds toasted, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) honey, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, 125 ml (½ cup) water, 2 stalks spring onion thinly sliced, Salt to taste
cooking_steps: Cut beef into medium-sized thin slices. Prick the meat with a fork several times until tender. Mix beef slices with blended ingredients together with honey, thick soy sauce, a little salt and sesame oil. Marinate for 1 hour or overnight in the refrigerator. Heat oil in a pan. Add marinated meat with its sauce into the pan and cook for 2 minutes. Add 1/2 cup water and reduce heat and simmer until meat is cooked and tender. Add more water if necessary. Place cooked meat into a bowl. Sprinkle with spring onion and sesame seeds. Place about 2 tablespoons of beef mixture onto each medium-sized lettuce leaf and roll. Serve immediately.
tips: Red meat is a good source of iron and is more easily absorbed by the body compared to iron from plant sources. Choose lean beef cuts to reduce fat intake in your diet.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: prawn, carrot, snow pea, capsicum, onion, ginger
ingredients_original: 200 g (20 pieces) prawns peeled and cleaned, 100 g (1 medium piece) carrot peeled sliced 1cm thick and blanched, 150 g snow peas blanched, 30 g (1 medium piece) capsicum roughly sliced, 50 g (2 pieces) onion roughly chopped, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) oil, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) lime juice, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) ginger chopped, Salt and white pepper to taste, Skewers
cooking_steps: Skewer prawns alternating with vegetables on skewers. In a separate bowl, mix oil, lime juice, ginger, salt and white pepper and brush onto kebabs. Place skewers in oven about 15 cm from heating element. Grill 3-4 minutes on each side, depending on size. Serve while hot.
tips: The fat in prawns is low (0.3g/100g). However, it is advised to remove the heads because they contain high cholesterol. Prawns can be replaced with fish chunks. Serve with BBQ sauce or any sauce of choice. Mushrooms, cherry tomatoes, and pineapples can also be included for variety and to increase fiber and color.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: egg, prawn, soft tofu, shiitake mushroom, broccoli, carrot
ingredients_original: 3 chicken eggs, 185 ml (¾ cup) homemade chicken stock, 3 large prawns cleaned and sliced, 75 g (1 block) soft tofu cut into small pieces and divided into 3, 9 g (4 medium pieces) shiitake mushroom thinly sliced, 35 g (3 tablespoons) broccoli chopped, 30 g (3 tablespoons) carrot chopped, Salt to taste, Garnish: spring onion chopped
cooking_steps: In a large bowl, beat eggs. Slowly pour chicken stock and salt to taste. In another container, mix mushroom, tofu, broccoli, carrot and prawn and divide into three bowls. Pour egg and chicken stock mixture into the 3 divided bowls. Boil water in a steamer. Reduce heat and place bowls containing egg mixture into steamer. Cover and steam for 20 minutes, or until egg is cooked and soft like tofu. Garnish each bowl with sliced spring onion and serve.
tips: Chopped ginkgo nuts and minced chicken meat can also be used as additional ingredients. It is recommended for healthy individuals to consume one egg a day. If you have high cholesterol, limit it to 2-3 eggs a week and it is advisable to separate the yolk which contains high cholesterol.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: salmon steak
ingredients_original: 600 g (6 pieces) Salmon steaks, 20 g (1 tablespoon) honey, 100 g (1 piece) lemon juice squeezed, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) olive oil, 15 g (1 tablespoon) BBQ sauce, Salt to taste, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) water, Garnish: Dill leaves/lemon
cooking_steps: Clean salmon, then place into a baking tray lined with baking paper. Then brush with olive oil. Mix water with lemon juice, salt, honey and BBQ sauce. Stir until well mixed. Smear mixture over salmon and coat evenly. Marinate for about 1 hour. Bake in oven for one hour at 150°C. Remove salmon from baking tray and place on a serving plate. Garnish with dill leaves or lemon and serve while hot with steamed vegetables, salad and sweet potato.
tips: Salmon contains Omega-3 fatty acids, Vitamin D and protein. Whether you buy fish fillets, fish steaks, or a whole fish, make sure the fish is still fresh and smells of the ocean. A fishy smell indicates the fish is no longer fresh and was not stored at an appropriate temperature. The body of a fresh fish is still firm and bounces back when pressed. Its skin and flesh do not tear easily, especially in the belly area. Its scales are still strong, shiny and not easily detached. The color of fresh gills is bright red, and the fish flesh is free from blood and fluids. Its eyes must be clear, bulging and convex. If cloudy or sunken into the eye socket, it means the fish is no longer fresh.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

recipe_name: Steamed Tilapia with Fermented Soybean Paste
ingredients: red tilapia, garlic, ginger, red chilli
ingredients_original: 300-400 g red tilapia fish cleaned, 20 g (1 tablespoon) fermented soybean paste (taucu) mashed, 5 g (1 clove) garlic finely chopped, 10 g (½ inch) ginger sliced, 7 ml (½ tablespoon) cooking oil, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) water, 4 g (½ piece) red chilli chopped (optional), 1 stalk spring onion chopped, 1 stalk coriander leaves chopped
cooking_steps: Heat cooking oil in a pan and sauté garlic and ginger before adding fermented soybean paste. Add water and stir until gravy starts to thicken. Turn off heat and add chopped chilli. Place fish in a heat-proof plate and pour gravy over it. Steam over boiling water for 10-12 minutes. Test if fish is cooked by pricking a small knife into the thickest part of the fish. If flesh parts easily, the fish is cooked. Serve hot with sliced spring onion and coriander leaves.
tips: Fermented soybean paste (taucu) is famous in Chinese and Indonesian cuisines. It is a paste made from preserved soybeans and tastes salty. When cooking with taucu, it is recommended to use only a little salt or better yet, do not use it at all.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: mackerel, lady's finger, red chilli, shallot, garlic, lemongrass, candlenut
ingredients_original: 600 g (5 medium pieces) mackerel cleaned, 60 g (5 pieces) lady's finger diagonally sliced, 4 pieces aluminum foil 20 cm x 20 cm, 1 wide banana leaf, 18 g (½ piece) lime juice squeezed, 30 g (4 pieces) red chillies, 80 g (8 pieces) shallots, 20 g (4 cloves) garlic, 60 g (2 stalks) lemongrass, 5 g (1 teaspoon) shrimp paste, 10 g (1 tablespoon) chilli powder, 45 g (4 pieces) candlenuts, 2 kaffir lime leaves, 250 ml (1 cup) water, 45 ml (3 tablespoons) oil, Salt and sugar to taste
cooking_steps: Smoke banana leaf over small fire until slightly soft. Cut banana leaf into several pieces enough to wrap fish. Set aside. Heat oil and sauté blended ingredients with candlenuts over medium heat until fragrant and slightly crisp. Add lime juice, kaffir lime leaf, sugar and salt to taste. Sauté until combined. Add a little water if necessary. Simmer for 2 minutes and cook until sambal is slightly thick. Add lady's finger and mix briefly. Turn off heat. Line banana leaf on aluminum foil. Arrange 2-3 fish on the banana leaf. Pour sambal over fish evenly. Cover fish with aluminum sheet and fold surroundings neatly so no air can escape. Repeat same step for other fish. Place fish wrapped in aluminum on a flat pan over medium heat. Cook en papillotte for about 10 minutes. Serve with hot rice.
tips: To eliminate the fishy smell, soak the fish in tamarind water for about 15 minutes, then rinse clean before cooking. It is recommended to eat 1 serving of fish every day. Choose oily fish like sardines, sea bass, hardtail scad, black pomfret, and mackerel because they contain unsaturated fatty acids (omega-3 fatty acids).
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: blue crab, egg, green capsicum, red chilli, garlic, ginger, shallot
ingredients_original: 1 kg (5 pieces) blue crab cleaned, 1 egg beaten, 50 g (½ piece) green capsicum sliced, 32 g (2 pieces) red chillies, 50 g (1 bulb) garlic, 20 g (1 inch) ginger, 60 g (6 pieces) shallots, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, 1 stalk coriander leaves chopped, 2 stalks spring onion cut into 1 inch, 45 ml (3 tablespoons) chilli sauce, 75 g (5 tablespoons) tomato sauce, 7 g (½ tablespoon) brown sugar, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) vinegar, 125 ml (½ cup) water, 1.25 g (½ teaspoon) corn flour dissolved in ¼ cup water, Salt and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pan and sauté blended ingredients until fragrant. Add crab and continue cooking. Add sauce and about 1/2 cup water. Cover pan and simmer for about 15 minutes, or until crab is cooked and gravy becomes thick. Add capsicum followed by coriander leaves. Stir and leave for a moment. Add beaten egg and stir quickly to prevent egg from clumping. Turn off heat. Remove and serve with sliced spring onion.
tips: Seafood generally contains lower calories compared to other animal protein source foods. However, crabs contain high cholesterol, so limit the choice of crab dishes.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: tilapia, galangal, carrot, cauliflower, broccoli, baby corn, red chilli, shallot, garlic, lemongrass
ingredients_original: 600 g (1 medium piece) tilapia fish cleaned, 50 g (½ inch) ginger sliced, 30 g (¼ medium piece) carrot cut lengthwise, 50 g (1 cup) cauliflower cut into small pieces, 50 g (1 cup) broccoli cut into small pieces, 50 g (1 cup) baby corn sliced diagonally, Garnish: mint leaves, 100 g (6 pieces) fresh red chillies, 120 g (12 cloves) shallot, 10 g (2 cloves) garlic, 30 g (1 stalk) lemongrass, 1 cm galangal, 35 g (2 tablespoons) tom yam paste, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) lime juice, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, 3 kaffir lime leaves, 125 ml (½ cup) water, Salt and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: To make tom yam paste, heat oil in a pan, sauté blended ingredients until fragrant. Add tom yam paste and continue sautéing. Add a little water. Add carrot, cauliflower, broccoli and corn one by one. Continue cooking for 5 minutes, and add kaffir lime leaf, ginger, salt and white pepper to taste. Stir briefly and turn off heat. Add lime juice. Place fish on a heat-proof plate and pour tom yam paste over it. Smear evenly over fish with vegetables. Place fish into steamer and steam until cooked for about 10 minutes. Serve while hot with mint leaf garnish.
tips: This cooking method is suitable for all types of fish. Tom yam spices will eliminate the fishy smell. Replace or add other vegetables such as tomatoes, long beans, and lady's fingers (okra).
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: egg, prawn, garlic
ingredients_original: 5 boiled eggs, 90 g (½ cup) prawns peeled boiled and chopped, 8 g (½ teaspoon) low-fat mayonnaise, 5 g (1 clove) garlic chopped, 2 stalks coriander leaves finely chopped, Black pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Peel egg shells. Rinse and drain. Cut eggs lengthwise into 2 parts. Carefully remove the yolk. Place egg yolks in a large bowl. Mix with mayonnaise and garlic. Mix well until combined. Add prawn, coriander leaves and black pepper into the egg yolk mixture. Mash mixture until soft. Fill each half of boiled egg white with the egg yolk mixture. Chill briefly before serving.
tips: Egg yolks are high in vitamin A, D, and iron content. Egg consumption is recommended for all healthy individuals. If fat intake from meat and dairy products is not excessive, there is no need to limit egg yolk intake. For variety, add finely chopped boiled peas, carrots, and celery.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: yee mee, chicken, prawn, black mushroom, carrot, broccoli, capsicum, garlic, onion, ginger
ingredients_original: 160 g (4 pieces) yellow yee mee blanched and drained, 100 g chicken breast cut into small pieces, 100 g (10 pieces) prawns head and tail removed, 20 g (10 pieces) black mushroom soaked and thinly sliced, 50 g (½ piece) carrot cut into long strips, 50 g (½ cup) broccoli cut into small pieces, 50 g (½ medium piece) capsicum thinly sliced, 250 ml (2 cups) water, 20 g (4 cloves) garlic crushed, 20 g (2 pieces) onion thinly sliced, 20 g (1 inch) ginger grated, 20 g (1 tablespoon) oyster sauce, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) thick soy sauce, 2.5 ml (½ teaspoon) sesame oil, 7 g (1 tablespoon) corn flour mixed evenly with a little water, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, Salt white pepper and black pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pan and sauté onion, garlic and ginger until fragrant and slightly crisp. Add chicken meat and sauté for a few minutes. Add 2 cups water and boil. Add thick soy sauce, oyster sauce, black pepper and sesame oil. Simmer for a few minutes. Add prawn, carrot, broccoli, capsicum and mushroom. Continue simmering until vegetables are tender. Add salt and white pepper to taste. Pour corn flour and stir slowly until gravy becomes thick. Turn off heat and pour gravy over yee mee. Serve while hot.
tips: Sizzling noodles are served on a hot plate. 'Sizzling' comes from the English word referring to the hissing sound when the noodles are cooked on a hot pan. This dish is a complete meal because it contains carbohydrates from noodles, protein from chicken and prawns, minerals, and vitamins from vegetables.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: rice, chicken breast, prawn, crab meat, cockle, mussel, celery stick, mixed vegetable, red capsicum, green capsicum, onion, garlic
ingredients_original: 480 g (2 cups) rice washed and drained, 150 g (½ cup) skinless chicken breast cut into small pieces, 400 g (4 cups) prawns peeled, 100 g crab meat, 60 g (6 pieces) cockles cleaned, 60 g (7 pieces) mussels cleaned, 30 g (¼ cup) celery stalk diced, 220 g (1 cup) mixed vegetables (corn, carrot and peas), 30 g (¼ cup) red and green capsicum diced, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) lemon juice, 26 g (1 piece) onion diced, 20 g (4 cloves) garlic chopped, 330 ml (1¾ cups) chicken stock, 3 g (½ teaspoon) sweet paprika powder, 7 g (½ tablespoon) turmeric powder, 1 stalk chinese celery chopped, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) olive oil, Salt and white pepper to taste, Garnish: lemon slices
cooking_steps: Heat olive oil in a medium large pot. Sauté onion, garlic, red capsicum, green capsicum and celery stick until crisp and browned. Add rice followed by paprika powder and turmeric. Pour chicken stock with chicken breast and let boil. Add crab meat, prawn, cockles and mussels. Mix well, then add salt and white pepper to taste to make paella. Cover paella and simmer over small fire for about 20 to 25 minutes until cooked. Then add mixed vegetables with lemon juice. Mix well and cook briefly until vegetables are slightly soft. Turn off heat and serve while hot with lemon slices and chinese celery.
tips: This recipe is good because it contains various types of low-fat seafood. It is also a complete dish because it contains carbohydrates from rice, protein and minerals from seafood, and vitamins from vegetables. Paella can be found in various types, depending on the available local ingredients. Therefore, it is known as a versatile food in Spain.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: minced meat, potato, carrot, button mushroom, milk, mozzarella cheese, onion, wheat flour
ingredients_original: 300 g (¾ packet) minced meat, 450 g (5 pieces) potatoes, 50 g (½ piece) carrot roughly chopped, 70 g (½ cup) button mushroom thinly sliced, 125 ml (½ cup) milk, 120 g (1 cup) Mozzarella cheese grated, 26 g (1 piece) onion sliced, 16 g (2 tablespoons) wheat flour/wholemeal flour dissolved in ½ cup water, 375 ml (1½ cups) homemade vegetable stock, 7 g (1 tablespoon) black pepper powder, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) soy sauce, 7 ml (½ tablespoon) honey, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, A few sprigs / a little thyme leaves powder, Salt and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Mix minced meat with black pepper powder, soy sauce, honey and thyme leaves. Marinate for 2 hours. Boil potatoes with skin. Once soft, peel skin and mash with milk and cheese. Set aside. Sauté onion with oil until fragrant and add marinated meat. Sauté until meat is cooked and no longer pink. Pour vegetable stock with carrot. Simmer until carrot is soft, then add mushroom. Add salt and white pepper to taste and continue simmering over slow fire. When meat is cooked, add flour solution. Mix well until mixture becomes thick. Turn off heat. Place cooked minced meat on a medium baking dish and level the surface. Cover meat surface with mashed potato until finished and covering the surface of baking tray. Use a fork to make patterns on the mashed potato. Bake in oven at 180°C for 25 minutes or until top of mashed potato becomes browned. Cut into several parts and serve hot.
tips: Before adding dried herbs to a dish, rub the herbs between your fingers to release the volatile oils. Add the herbs in the early stages of cooking so their flavors can be well absorbed into the dish. Add other vegetables like peas, celery stalks, and corn to increase nutrient content. A good way to encourage your child to eat meat is to mince it, making it easier for children to eat.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: yellow noodle, rice vermicelli, water spinach, bean sprout, tomato, egg, chicken, prawn, fresh chilli, shallot, garlic
ingredients_original: 300 g (3 cups) yellow noodle blanched, 150 g (½ piece) rice vermicelli soaked and blanched, 300 g (4 cups) water spinach hard stems removed and blanched, 500 g (3 cups) bean sprouts blanched, 50 g (2 medium pieces) tomato quartered, 3 hard boiled eggs peeled and halved, Garnish: Fried onion (optional), 200 g lean chicken with bone, 150 g prawns with head and shell, 2.5 liters (10 cups) water, Salt to taste, 10 g dried chilli deseeded and blanched, 25 g (3 pieces) fresh chillies, 3 tablespoons dried shrimp, 50 g (5 pieces) shallot, 15 g (3 cloves) garlic, 45 ml (3 tablespoons) cooking oil, 250 ml (1 cup) water, Brown sugar to taste
cooking_steps: To make chilli paste, blend chilli paste ingredients until fine and combined. Heat oil in a pan. Sauté chilli paste briefly and add water. Continue cooking for about 10 minutes over medium heat until water is slightly dried and oil separates. Stir constantly. Add brown sugar and mix briefly. Turn off heat and set aside. Pour water into a pot and boil. Add prawn and chicken. Simmer over medium heat for about 1.5 hours or until stock becomes cloudy. Remove prawn and chicken and transfer to a plate. Let cool briefly and remove prawn heads and shells. Separate chicken meat from bone and slice chicken meat thinly. Boil the stock again and add a few spoons of chilli paste, according to spicy preference. Add salt to taste and turn off heat. Finally add tomato. To serve, place a ladle of noodles and vermicelli, water spinach and bean sprouts in a bowl. Pour gravy and garnish with sliced chicken meat, prawn, egg and fried onion. Serve while hot.
tips: This recipe is a complete meal. Eggs, prawns, and chicken are protein sources. Vegetables like water spinach and bean sprouts are sources of minerals and vitamins, while carbohydrates are supplied by yellow noodles and rice vermicelli.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: minced beef, lasagna sheet, carrot, spinach, mozzarella cheese, parmesan cheese, olive, garlic, tomato, yellow onion
ingredients_original: 200 g (½ packet) minced beef, 8-10 lasagna sheets, 50 g (½ piece) carrot cut lengthwise, 60 g (1½ cups) spinach chopped, 90 g (1½ cups) Mozzarella cheese grated, 90 g (3 cups) Parmesan cheese grated (optional), 140 g preserved olives sliced (optional), 15 g (3 cloves) garlic chopped, 4 g fried onion, 125 ml (1 cup) warm water, 130 g (5 pieces) ripe tomato blended, 50 g (2 pieces) yellow onion peeled and diced, 25 g (5 cloves) garlic chopped, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) olive oil, 30 g (2 tablespoons) dried herb mixture (basil, oregano, rosemary, thyme, etc.), 250 ml (1 cup) water, Salt brown sugar and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: To make Marinara sauce, heat olive oil in a pot. Sauté onion over medium heat until browned and soft. Add garlic, dried herb mixture and sauté until fragrant. Pour water and tomatoes with juice and stir until combined. Simmer over slow fire, stirring until gravy becomes thick. Add salt, brown sugar and white pepper to taste. Turn off heat, sprinkle fried onion and let cool. To make meat filling, fry meat with garlic over medium heat with a little oil. Then add water and simmer until meat is cooked and tender. Add carrot, spinach and olives. Cook until vegetables are soft and stir constantly. Add Marinara sauce and stir until combined. Turn off heat and remove from pot. Preheat oven to 190°C. Layer bottom of a medium baking bowl with meat filling, then cover with a sprinkle of a little Parmesan cheese with Mozzarella cheese. Cover with dried lasagna. Repeat layers with meat filling, cheese and lasagna until all ingredients are used. Save 1/2 cup Mozzarella cheese for the final surface layer. Bake for 45 minutes in oven until cooked, depending on thickness of lasagna layers. Lasagna is cooked when a knife pierces into it easily. Sprinkle remaining cheese on the final layer, and put back into oven for about 10 minutes. Serve while hot.
tips: Beef can also be replaced with chicken meat. You can also substitute or add more vegetables such as peas, brinjal, and broccoli. Beef is a good source of protein and iron, while cheese is a good source of calcium. This recipe is not only suitable for those who like western food, but it is also nutritious for your whole family.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: brown rice, fish fillet, spinach leaf, tomato, onion
ingredients_original: 480 g (2 cups) brown rice washed and drained, 150 g (½ cup) fish fillet grilled and sliced, 300 g (5 cups) spinach leaves hard stems removed and chopped, 50 g (2 pieces) tomato diced, 6 stalks spring onion thinly sliced, 26 g (1 piece) onion sliced, 3 g (1 teaspoon) cardamom powder, 4 stalks coriander leaves chopped, 70 g (½ piece) lemon juice squeezed, 500 ml (2 cups) water, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, 2.5 ml (½ teaspoon) sesame oil, Salt and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pot and sauté onion until fragrant and browned. Add spinach into the pot with a few spoons of water. Slowly stir spinach, add cardamom powder and tomato with a pinch of salt and white pepper to taste. Cook for another 2 minutes and then add rice together with 2 cups water. Cook over medium heat until rice is cooked. Reduce heat and add fish, sesame oil, lemon juice, spring onion and chopped coriander leaves. Mix until combined. Turn off heat. Serve hot in a plate.
tips: Serve with a chicken or fish dish to make a complete meal. If your family members do not like vegetables, this recipe is a smart way to encourage vegetable consumption.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

recipe_name: Penne Pasta with Brinjal and Cheese
original_recipe_name: Pasta Penne dengan Terung dan Keju
ingredients: penne pasta, chicken meat, brinjal, cheese, tomato, green capsicum, garlic
ingredients_original: 250 g (¾ packet) Penne pasta, 120 g (1 cup) chicken meat cut into cubes, 300 g (2 pieces) brinjal cut into 2 cm cubes, 60 g (½ cup) grated cheese, 100 g (4 pieces) tomato diced, 30 g (½ piece) green capsicum thinly sliced, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) olive oil, 180 ml (¾ cup) homemade chicken stock, 10 g (2 cloves) garlic pounded, 1 stalk parsley chopped, Salt and black pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Cook penne pasta in boiling water. Drain and set aside. Place brinjal in a large colander and sprinkle with a little salt. Leave for about 30 minutes. Remove excess liquid before baking. Grill brinjal on a flat pan with a little oil. When brinjal becomes brown, remove from pan and set aside. Sauté onion until browned. Add chicken and sauté until cooked. Add capsicum and tomato, then add chicken stock. Simmer for 5 minutes. Add brinjal and parsley leaf into the sauce and continue simmering for 2 minutes. Add salt and black pepper to taste, then reduce heat. Mix pasta into the sauce and sprinkle cheese. Stir briefly and turn off heat. Serve while hot.
tips: Choose whole wheat pasta as it has higher fiber and nutrients. Penne (PEN-nay) is one of the popular pasta shapes, especially in the Campania region of southern Italy. Penne comes from the Italian word meaning quill pen. It is usually 2 inches wide and straight-tubed cut diagonally resembling the tip of a chicken feather.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: potato, fish fillet, milk, cheddar cheese, green pea, onion, wholemeal flour, egg
ingredients_original: 270 g (3 medium pieces) potatoes, 180 g (1 cup) fish fillet minced, 30 g (2 tablespoons) butter, 125 ml (½ cup) milk, 60 g (½ cup) Cheddar cheese grated, 50 ml (⅕ cup) homemade vegetable stock, 30 g (2 tablespoons) green peas, 25 g (1 piece) onion finely chopped, 8 g (1 tablespoon) wholemeal flour, 3 g (1 tablespoon) parsley leaves chopped, 1 basil leaf, 1 egg beaten, Salt and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Preheat oven to 180°C. Boil water in a pot containing a little salt and boil potatoes for 15-20 minutes. Remove potatoes and peel skin. Mash with a little milk and half the butter until soft. Melt remaining butter in a pot, add onion and sauté until crisp. Add flour and cook for 30 minutes. Pour remaining milk slowly into the bowl, followed by vegetable stock. Boil and leave for 1 minute. Add fish, parsley leaf and basil leaf. Cook for 3 minutes then add green peas and cook again for 1 minute. Remove basil leaf and add cheese until cheese melts. Sprinkle white pepper and salt to taste. Pour fish filling into baking bowl and cover with mashed potato. Brush with a little beaten egg. Bake in preheated oven for 15 minutes until golden yellow. Garnish pie with vegetables.
tips: This pie is also suitable for toddlers aged 1-2 years. Leftover pie can be frozen.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: tuna, potato, wholemeal breadcrumb, corn kernel, egg
ingredients_original: 180 g (1 can) tuna in water drained and mashed, 180 g (2 large pieces) potatoes boiled and peeled, 100 g (1 cup) wholemeal breadcrumbs, 60 g (¼ cup) fresh corn kernels, 2 eggs, 3 g (1 teaspoon) coriander seeds finely pounded, Salt and white pepper to taste, Enough cooking oil, Garnish: Lemon and cucumber slices or fresh salad leaves (optional)
cooking_steps: Mash boiled potatoes in a bowl. Mix tuna, corn kernels, and coriander. Add salt and white pepper to taste. Shape into 7 croquette pieces. Beat egg in a large bowl. Dip croquette pieces into egg mixture immediately and coat with breadcrumbs. Fry in hot oil until croquette pieces become browned. Drain excess oil and place croquette on absorbent paper. Serve immediately with sliced cucumber or salad leaves with lemon and chilli sauce.
tips: Tuna is sold in various types in the market. It is recommended to choose tuna in water without salt compared to those soaked in oil. You can also bake the croquettes in the oven at 180°C for 15 minutes.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: wholemeal bread, chicken ham, egg, cheese, spinach, onion, self-raising flour
ingredients_original: 4 pieces wholemeal bread, 45 g (1 piece) chicken ham chopped, 4 eggs, 60 g (½ cup) cheese grated, 30 g (½ cup) spinach chopped, 15 g (1 tablespoon) butter, 10 g (1 tablespoon) onion chopped, 10 g (1 tablespoon) self-raising flour, 2 stalks coriander leaves finely chopped, Salt and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Remove bread crusts. Spread bread with a little butter. Arrange bread in a medium bowl with buttered side facing up. Place ham in a separate bowl. Mix spinach, onion, egg, coriander leaf, cheese and flour into that bowl. Stir evenly using a fork. Add salt and white pepper to taste. Pour egg mixture over bread arrangement. Place in microwave oven and cook for 8 minutes or bake in oven for 15 minutes at 190-200°C. Prick quiche with a knife to ensure it is cooked enough. If cooked enough, knife can be removed cleanly. Serve hot or cold.
tips: Whole wheat bread contains essential B-complex vitamins such as pantothenic acid (vitamin B5), pyridoxine (vitamin B6), thiamine (vitamin B1) and niacin (B3). These vitamins are essential for fat, protein, and carbohydrate metabolism in the body. The body cannot produce them on its own, so it needs to get them from other sources, namely food.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: wholemeal pita bread, meat, cucumber, onion, tomato, black mushroom, lettuce leaf
ingredients_original: 3 pieces wholemeal pita bread, 200 g (1 cup) meat fat removed and minced, 40 g (¼ piece) cucumber thinly sliced, 25 g (1 piece) onion chopped, 25 g (1 piece) tomato thinly sliced, 10 g (5 pieces) dried black chinese mushroom soaked and thinly sliced, A few lettuce leaves, 2 g (½ teaspoon) black pepper powder, 3 g (1 teaspoon) curry powder, 5 g (1 teaspoon) spice mixture (cinnamon, cumin, etc.), Salt to taste, A little oil for frying, Low-fat mayonnaise and chilli sauce (optional)
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pan and sauté onion until fragrant. Add spice mixture, curry powder and black pepper until fragrant. Add meat and sauté until slightly dry. Add salt and white pepper to taste until meat is cooked. Add a little water if too dry. Add black mushroom and continue cooking until mushroom becomes soft. Heat pita bread on a flat pan over slow fire. Cut pita bread in half and split the inside of the bread to make a pocket for inserting meat filling. Insert clean lettuce and arrange cucumber and tomato slices into the pita bread pocket. Insert a spoonful of meat filling into the pita bread pocket and level it between vegetables. Place chilli sauce and mayonnaise on top of meat filling and serve.
tips: Choose pita bread made from whole wheat flour which is rich in fiber. Yogurt can be used to replace mayonnaise because it is low in fat and high in calcium.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: minced meat, oat, garlic, onion, ginger, egg
ingredients_original: 400 g (1 packet) lean minced meat, 86 g (1 cup) oats, 20 g (4 cloves) garlic, 26 g (1 piece) onion, 10 g (½ inch) ginger, 1 egg, 5 ml (1 teaspoon) light soy sauce, A little dried herb mixture (basil, oregano, rosemary, thyme, etc.) (optional), 1 stalk coriander leaves chopped, Salt and white pepper to taste, Aluminum foil
cooking_steps: Mix all ingredients together in a bowl until combined. Add salt and white pepper to taste. Shape mixture into thin burger shapes and place on aluminum foil. Next, place into a container. Repeat steps until mixture is used up. Keep in refrigerator overnight. Fry with a little oil until the outside of the meat becomes brown. Then bake it in oven for 10-15 minutes until cooked.
tips: Serve with whole wheat bread, salad, sliced cucumber and tomato, ketchup, and cheese.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: macaroni, chicken breast, broccoli, milk, cheddar cheese, flour
ingredients_original: 130 g (1½ cups) macaroni, 120 g (½ cup) skinless chicken breast sliced, 70 g (¾ cup) broccoli finely chopped, 250 ml (1 cup) low-fat milk, 60 g (½ cup) grated Cheddar/Mozzarella cheese, 15 g (1 tablespoon) butter, 10 g (1 tablespoon) flour, 15 ml (1 tablespoon) oil, Salt and white pepper to taste, A pinch of dried basil powder (optional)
cooking_steps: Cook macaroni in boiling water until soft and drain. Heat oil in a pan and sauté chicken until half cooked. Add broccoli and continue cooking until soft. Turn off heat and separate. For white sauce, melt butter in a pan over medium heat and mix milk with flour. Wait until it starts to boil and mix in cheese. Then, stir until melted and combined. Add chicken with broccoli and macaroni, continue stirring. Add salt, white pepper and dried basil powder to taste. Stir for 2 minutes until cooked enough. Let cool briefly and serve.
tips: Adding vegetables to pasta can encourage picky children to enjoy new flavors. Try varying flavor and color combinations!
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: potato, chicken breast, tomato, cheese
ingredients_original: 270 g (3 medium pieces) potatoes (preferably Russet type), 120 g (½ cup) chicken breast fat and skin removed grilled and chopped, 25 g (1 piece) tomato chopped, 2 tablespoons grated cheese, 2 stalks spring onion chopped, A little dried basil leaf powder, Salt and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Wash and scrub potatoes until clean. Dry and prick the whole potato skin with a fork. Wrap loosely using damp cloth. Place into microwave oven and cook for 10 minutes. Can also bake the potato in an oven at 180°C for 1 hour. Meanwhile, mix tomato, minced chicken and chopped spring onion in a small bowl with grated cheese and sprinkle a little basil leaf. Sprinkle a little salt and white pepper to taste. Remove potato from microwave oven. When potato is cool, cut the top part to become a lid. Scoop out potato flesh and leave excess flesh 1 cm thick shaped like a boat. Mix the scooped potato flesh with tomato mixture, and insert a little filling into the potato boat. Place into microwave oven and cook for 1 minute or in oven for 2 minutes at 200°C, or until top becomes golden brown. Remove from oven and serve with salad.
tips: Eat potatoes with their skin, because it has high fiber content and is good for the digestive system.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: popiah skin, banana, peanut butter, peanut
ingredients_original: 4 pieces spring roll wrappers steamed (preferably use wet ones), 200 g (2 pieces) bananas halved, 50 g (2 tablespoons) peanut butter, 30 ml (2 tablespoons) honey, 30 g (1 tablespoon) peanuts crushed, 2.5 g (1 teaspoon) corn flour mixed with ½ cup water
cooking_steps: Place banana in the middle of wrapper. Spread peanut butter on banana. Add a little honey on peanut butter, followed by crushed peanuts. Roll the wrapper. Next brush corn flour on the fold flap to strengthen the wrap. Keep in plastic bag and freeze overnight (at least 4 hours to ensure banana is truly frozen). Cut according to preferred size and serve cold.
tips: No specific tip provided.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: biscuit, yogurt, lettuce leaf, carrot, cheese
ingredients_original: 8 pieces small-sized biscuits, 125 g (½ cup) yogurt, 54 g (1 cup) lettuce leaves cut to biscuit size, 30 g (¼ cup) carrot grated, 36 g (1 piece) cheese divided into 8 portions
cooking_steps: Mix yogurt with grated carrot. Place lettuce leaf on biscuit. Spread a thin layer of yogurt and carrot on salad. Then place another piece of biscuit on the yogurt layer. Then, place a piece of cheese on the biscuit followed by salad and finally a thin layer of yogurt with carrot. Serve.
tips: There are many types of biscuits in the market. Make a smart choice by choosing biscuits made from whole wheat flour, as well as those low in salt, sugar, and fat.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: chicken meat, cornflake, egg, wheat flour
ingredients_original: 360 g (1½ cups) chicken meat skin and bones removed, 200 g (2 cups) cornflakes crushed, 1 egg, 8 g (½ tablespoon) mustard, 1 teaspoon honey, A little wheat flour, Salt and white pepper to taste, Chilli or tomato sauce (optional)
cooking_steps: Preheat oven to 230°C. Cut chicken into 4-inch long strips. Coat chicken strips with flour. In a bowl, mix egg, honey, salt and mustard. In another bowl, mix cornflakes with white pepper. Dip chicken strips into egg mixture. Then, insert chicken strips into cornflake mixture and coat evenly. Arrange chicken strips on a baking tray lined with paper liner. Bake in oven for 12 minutes, or until chicken strips become golden yellow. Serve with chilli or tomato sauce.
tips: Use a paper liner/parchment paper on the baking tray to prevent the bottom of the chicken fingers from burning.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: pita bread, cheese, cucumber, tomato, shallot
