ingredients_original: 1 packet pita bread/tortilla, 120 g (1 cup) grated cheese, 135 g (½ piece) small cucumber thinly sliced, 25 g (1 piece) tomato thinly sliced, 25 g (1 piece) shallot chopped, 13 g (1 tablespoon) olive oil, 15 g (1 tablespoon) butter, 8 g (1 teaspoon) dried thyme (optional), Salt and white pepper to taste
cooking_steps: Preheat oven to 200°C. Heat oil and butter in a pan under medium heat until butter melts. Add onion and cucumber, continue cooking and stirring until vegetables are cooked. Add thyme, salt and white pepper to taste. Place pita bread/tortilla on a baking tray. Pour onion filling mixture evenly on pita bread/tortilla, and leave 1-2 inches at the end. Sprinkle tomato and cheese evenly on filling mixture. Bake for 15 minutes or until pita bread/tortilla is browned and cheese melts. Remove from oven and serve.
tips: Cheese is rich in calcium and is needed for children's bone health to support their physical growth.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: wheat flour, corn flour, whole cornflake cereal, whole wheat cereal, dried apricot, egg, cashew nut, almond, cherry
ingredients_original: 175 g (1½ cups) wheat flour, 60 g (½ cup) corn flour, 60 g (2 cups) whole cornflake cereal finely ground, 60 g (2 cups) whole wheat cereal finely ground, 150 g (1 cup) dried apricot chopped, 150 g (¾ cup) butter or margarine, 100 g (½ cup) caster sugar, 5 g (1 teaspoon) baking powder, 5 ml (1 teaspoon) vanilla essence, 1 egg, For variety: garnish with nuts (e.g. cashew nuts, almonds) or cherries
cooking_steps: Sift together wheat flour, corn flour and baking powder. Add whole cornflake cereal, whole wheat cereal and chopped apricot. Mix well and set aside. Mix butter, sugar and vanilla essence until it becomes a soft cream. Add egg and beat until combined. Add flour into butter mixture and mix well until it forms a dough. Flatten dough to 1 cm thick and cut using cookie cutter with preferred shape. Arrange dough on a baking tray greased with a little butter. Garnish with cherry. Bake in preheated oven at 180°C for 18-20 minutes or until golden brown. Remove from oven and let cool for 20 minutes.
tips: No specific tip provided.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: self-raising flour, walnut, carrot, milk, egg
ingredients_original: 240 g (1¾ cups) self-raising flour, ½ cup walnut ground, 120 g (1 cup) carrot grated, 125 ml (½ cup) fresh milk or yogurt, 2 eggs lightly beaten, 120 g (½ cup) butter, 3 g (1 teaspoon) baking powder, 3 g (1 teaspoon) cinnamon powder, 100 g (½ cup) brown sugar, 2 ml (½ teaspoon) vanilla essence or real vanilla
cooking_steps: Preheat oven to 190°C. Grease baking tray with a little butter or oil and line with muffin cups. Sift flour, baking powder and cinnamon powder into a bowl. Add egg, butter and milk and beat briefly until combined and soft. In another bowl, mix brown sugar, grated carrot, walnut and vanilla essence. Stir until combined. Pour egg mixture into carrot mixture. Mix well using a wooden spoon until mixture becomes slightly lumpy and not too runny. Pour batter into muffin tray and bake for 20 minutes or until wooden tester comes out clean. Cool muffin tray for 5 minutes before transferring to cake rack.
tips: Sprinkle walnuts and grated carrots as a garnish and low-fat cream cheese as icing. Carrots are rich in Vitamin A, which is essential for eye health. Walnuts are rich in omega-3.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: milk, banana, persone juice, yogurt
ingredients_original: 250 g (1 cup) low-fat milk, 200 g (2 medium sized) ripe bananas cut to ¼ inch thick, 125 ml (½ cup) fresh squeezed persone juice, 150 g (1 cup) yogurt, Brown sugar to taste
cooking_steps: Put all ingredients into a blender. Cover and blend for 45 to 60 seconds until smooth. Pour into a glass and serve cold.
tips: Suitable for children who do not really like the taste of regular milk! The consumption of milk and dairy products is encouraged because they are rich sources of essential nutrients, including protein, retinol, riboflavin, vitamin D, vitamin B12, zinc, magnesium, and calcium.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: mango, persone, pineapple, papaya, guava
ingredients_original: 230 g (1 medium piece) ripe mango, 135 g (1 piece) persone, 130 g (1 slice) pineapple, 600 g (1 medium piece) papaya, 325 g (1 medium piece) guava, 250 ml (1 cup) water, 8 ml (½ tablespoon) honey (optional), A few ice cubes
cooking_steps: Peel fruits and remove all seeds. Blend fruits with water. Add honey to taste. Add ice cubes and serve immediately.
tips: This recipe is good for your children so they can consume more fruits rich in vitamins and minerals needed for their growth and development. You can create new flavors by mixing your children's favorite fruits together.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga

ingredients: soy milk, banana, apple, kiwi
ingredients_original: 250 ml (1 cup) soy milk, 100 g (1 piece) banana peeled and cut, 100 g (1 piece) apple deseeded, 179 g (1 piece) kiwi peeled
cooking_steps: Blend all ingredients together and serve cold.
tips: Can be substituted with other local fruits like honeydew melon, watermelon, and so on. Soybeans are high in protein, fiber, and unsaturated fat, as well as rich in vitamins and minerals.
source_book: Buku Masakan Pilihan Pakar Pemakanan Jilid 2: Resipi untuk Seisi Keluarga
